\chapter{About}
\label{\detokenize{about:about}}\label{\detokenize{about::doc}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Features:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compact size requires very little space on a breadboard;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
connects to the power rails on a breadboard;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
can be powered by USB\sphinxhyphen{}C port or external power supply;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
3.3V and 5V power supplies;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
GPIOs arranged into 4 groups for easier identification;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
on\sphinxhyphen{}board ESP32\sphinxhyphen{}C3 Module with USB\sphinxhyphen{}C port allows to flash a firmware as you work with breadboard;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
debugging points;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
power pads makes it easy to connect alligators supplying voltage to the board;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
on\sphinxhyphen{}board system LEDs: power, and module reset mode;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
on\sphinxhyphen{}board push switch for Reset and Flash modes;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
on\sphinxhyphen{}board push switch and LED for your needs;

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
ESP32\sphinxhyphen{}C3 Module:
* single\sphinxhyphen{}core
* 32\sphinxhyphen{}bit RISC\sphinxhyphen{}V MCU @ 160 MHz
* 400 KB of internal RAM
* RSA\sphinxhyphen{}3072\sphinxhyphen{}based secure boot and the AES\sphinxhyphen{}128/256\sphinxhyphen{}XTS flash encryption
* low power\sphinxhyphen{}mode support
* Rich connectivity for IoT applications: Wi\sphinxhyphen{}Fi and Bluetooth 5 (LE) with long\sphinxhyphen{}range support
* Bluetooth LE SIG Mesh and Wi\sphinxhyphen{}FI Mesh support
* 22 GPIOs

\sphinxstepscope

\section{MicroPython Blinky Example}
\label{\detokenize{examples:micropython-blinky-example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\},numbers=left,firstnumber=1,stepnumber=1]
\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{machine}
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{machine} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Pin}
\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{time}\PYG{o}{,} \PYG{n+nn}{math}

\PYG{n}{ONBOARD\PYGZus{}LED} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} GPIO10, PIN 7}
\PYG{n}{ONBOARD\PYGZus{}BTN} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}     \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} GPIO3, 13}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Configure on\PYGZhy{}board LED and push button}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Stated GPIOs correspond to the wiring schematic}
\PYG{n}{onboard\PYGZus{}button} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Pin}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ONBOARD\PYGZus{}BTN}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Pin}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{IN}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Pin}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{PULL\PYGZus{}UP}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{led} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{machine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{PWM}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ONBOARD\PYGZus{}LED}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{freq}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1000}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{def} \PYG{n+nf}{pulse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{l}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{t}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{i} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{l}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{duty}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{math}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{sin}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{i}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{n}{math}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{pi}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{500} \PYG{o}{+} \PYG{l+m+mi}{500}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{n}{time}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{sleep\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{l}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{duty}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Interrupt function to turn LED ON when on\PYGZhy{}board button is pressed}
\PYG{k}{def} \PYG{n+nf}{button\PYGZus{}interrupt}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{pin}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{pulse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{led}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{70}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{def} \PYG{n+nf}{main}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}

    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Assign interrupt to the on\PYGZhy{}board push button}
    \PYG{n}{onboard\PYGZus{}button}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{irq}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{trigger}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Pin}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{IRQ\PYGZus{}FALLING}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{handler}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{button\PYGZus{}interrupt}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n+nv+vm}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}name\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}main\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{main}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
